summary: AS050 is a LARGE-SCALE EVENT REGISTER with CORPUS-RECORD ASCHER CASCADE DEPTH 6 (deepest Merkle tree in the analysed corpus). 370 cords = 3rd-largest document analysed after AS069 (1831 cords) and UR190 (417 cords); BIGGEST EMPTY FRACTION on large scale: 210 of 370 cords EMPTY (57 percent). 1 STRING only (taqa at single position = boundary marker), 159 INT. 17 CLUSTERS. 5 colors: B(182 49%) + BD(95 26%) + W(39) + GY(20) + BY(17). KFG twin KH0061. CORPUS-RECORD ASCHER: 55 pendant-pendant sums declared, 42/48 HEADER VERIFIED = 88 percent integrity at MAX CASCADE DEPTH 6 -- exceeds AS092's cascade depth 5 (previous corpus max). 42 verified sums at depth 6 makes AS050 the DEEPEST-TREE AUDIT DOCUMENT analysed. 1 Pass 3C propagation unlocked. HYPOTHESIS: large-scale sparse EVENT REGISTER with audit-grade verification on a 6-level cascading Merkle structure. Single taqa STRING = cadastral boundary anchor; 159 INT numerical events distributed across 160 filled positions + 210 prepared-but-unfilled slot positions. Structurally unique: extreme scale (370 cords) + extreme sparsity (57 percent empty) + extreme cascade depth (6) on audit-verified arithmetic. Probably tracks events at an administrative site with multi-year or multi-decade timespan, where most slot positions remained pre-formatted reserves.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  370 cords. 159 INT, 1 STRING (taqa), 210 EMPTY (57 percent -- CORPUS-RECORD empty fraction for large docs).
  Architecture: 1-PAIRE, schema RELATIONAL depth L2.
  17 clusters.
  5 colors: B(182, 49%), BD(95, 26%), W(39, 11%), GY(20, 5%), BY(17, 5%).
  Provenance: UNKNOWN.
  KFG twin KH0061.

DATA QUALITY (v3)
  1/1 STRING at 100 percent dict hit (taqa).
  All 159 INT dict-stable. 210 pre-formatted slot positions.

CORPUS-RECORD ASCHER LAYER
  55 PENDANT-PENDANT SUMS DECLARED (one of highest in corpus).
  48 HEADER cords declared; 42/48 VERIFIED = 88 PERCENT INTEGRITY = AUDIT-GRADE.
  MAX CASCADE DEPTH 6 = NEW CORPUS RECORD (previous max was AS092's cascade depth 5).
  1 Pass 3C propagation (unique-unknown constraint solved).
  Covering ratio = 55 / ~160 L1 = 0.34 MEDIUM (approaching HIGH threshold 0.50).
  The combination of LARGE SCALE + HIGH SPARSITY + DEEP CASCADE + HIGH VERIFICATION is exceptional:
  AS050 is an extensive event register whose ARITHMETIC BACKBONE verifies at 88 percent at depth 6.

VOCABULARY (1 STRING, 100 percent dict hit)
  taqa x1 -- BOUNDARY (single cadastral anchor). The only lexical content on a 370-cord register.
  The single STRING marker suggests AS050 is PRIMARILY NUMERICAL with one boundary-marking
  contextual anchor, similar to UR263's single chaqa delivery-marker (but at much larger scale).

STRUCTURAL SIGNATURE -- LARGE SCALE + EXTREME SPARSITY
  370 cords total.
  57 PERCENT EMPTY (210/370) is exceptional for a large document. Most large registers (UR190 at
  14 percent, UR262 at 23 percent) have far fewer empties. The 57-percent sparsity places AS050
  in the same class as UR273A (74 percent empty at 87 cords) + UR192 (54 percent at 70 cords) but
  at 4-5x the cord count.
  17 clusters averaging 22 cords each, varying widely.
  159 filled INT cords + 210 empty = 160 filled positions in a 370-slot framework.
  Prepared-but-unfilled template convention applied at unusually large scale.

COLOR ROLE MAP
  B (182, 49%) = PRIMARY CHANNEL -- carries taqa + majority of INT data.
  BD (95, 26%) = SECONDARY (brown-dark) -- substantial channel for structural detail.
  W (39, 11%) = REFERENCE DATA.
  GY (20), BY (17) = specialty channels (grey, beige-yellow).
  5 colors on 370 cords = 1 color per 74 cords on average -- wide per-colour distribution
  consistent with category-encoded positions.

HYPOTHESIS
  AS050 is a LARGE-SCALE SPARSE EVENT REGISTER with AUDIT-GRADE ARITHMETIC VERIFICATION at the
  corpus's deepest Merkle cascade (depth 6). The single taqa STRING anchors the register to a
  cadastral boundary; the 370-cord scale + 57 percent empty + 5-color palette structure accommodates
  a long time-span or multi-category event-tracking framework. 42 verified sums across 48 headers
  at depth 6 provides cross-verification at multiple hierarchical levels -- the arithmetic Merkle
  tree cascades 6 levels deep, each level's sums verifying against its parent's.
  Without further STRING anchors, classification between event_register / audit_register / compound-
  document-component cannot be fully resolved. Most parsimonious reading: AS050 is a LONG-RUNNING
  ADMINISTRATIVE EVENT LOG with audit-verified arithmetic framework, possibly spanning multiple
  years or administrative cycles at an unknown Inca site.
narrative:
Three hundred and seventy cords. Two hundred and ten of them empty. One hundred and fifty-nine numerical. One word -- taqa -- on a single cord, naming a boundary. And the arithmetic reaches six levels deep.

This is the corpus's deepest Ascher Merkle tree. Previous record: AS092 at Munich, cascade depth five, eighteen-of-eighteen verification on a ten-by-six audit grid. AS050 breaks the record at depth six, with forty-two of forty-eight headers verifying -- eighty-eight percent -- across fifty-five declared pendant-pendant sums. An auditing scribe built a six-level hierarchical cross-verification structure: each level's sums cross-checking against the level above, each propagating upward to a root that holds the grand total.

And across the three hundred and seventy cords, one single word. Taqa, at an unspecified position, on one of the colour-B cords that dominate the document at forty-nine percent. The boundary. The cadastral anchor. That is the entire lexical content of AS050. The rest is structure, numbers, empties, and the six-level Merkle cascade.

Fifty-seven percent of the register is empty. Two hundred and ten of three hundred and seventy cords carry no knots. This is an EXTRAORDINARILY SPARSE register at extraordinary scale. UR273A at Incahuasi is seventy-four percent empty but at eighty-seven cords; UR192 at Huaca Perez is fifty-four percent empty at seventy cords. AS050's three hundred and seventy cords at fifty-seven percent empty preserves the same structural design at five-times the scale: a PRE-FORMATTED TEMPLATE with two hundred and ten slot positions awaiting entries that did not come.

What comes INSTEAD is the arithmetic. Fifty-five pendant-pendant sums across forty-eight declared headers. Forty-two verify. The cascade goes down six levels. This is not a day-book (day-books verify at ten-to-thirty percent). This is an audit register. But the audit is LIGHT: it audits the scarce events that actually occurred in a large prepared framework, rather than aggregating all transactions in a dense log.

Seventeen clusters, averaging twenty-two cords each. Five colours with colour-B dominant at forty-nine percent, brown-dark at twenty-six, white at eleven, grey at five, beige-yellow at five. The palette has internal category differentiation; the taqa STRING cord sits on B (the dominant channel), anchoring the register's boundary subject.

AS050 is structurally unique. Large. Sparse. Deep. Audit-verified. These four features together do not appear in any other analysed document. UR262 at Incahuasi Kallanka is large (490 cords) but not particularly sparse (23 percent empty) and has zero Ascher verification. UR273B is deep in per-cord audit magnitude but at smaller scale (124 cords). UR183 has the corpus-record peak value 8300 but small scale (33 cords). AS050 holds the unique position of LARGE AUDIT-VERIFIED SPARSE REGISTER at depth six.

Provenance unknown. The OKR does not record a specific site. What we have is KH0061 at the Khipu Field Guide, AS050 in the Ascher catalogue, and a field-investigator numbering that places it among the Ascher early catalogue khipus (low AS numbers typically correspond to early-published documents from North American collections). Whatever Inca administrator tied these three hundred and seventy cords, he built a six-level cross-verification cascade into them and sealed the arithmetic at eighty-eight percent integrity. The single word taqa tells us only that boundaries were the subject, not which boundaries at which site in which year.

prior_work.claim: Ascher pendant-pendant sum conventions establish a Merkle-tree-like hierarchical cross-verification structure; cascade depth is the longest DAG path in the dependency tree and caps practical erasure-correction capacity (Sivan 2026 T6 test: cascade depth does NOT discriminate audit capacity; covering ratio does).
prior_work.citation: Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes. ALBA Project preprint; Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1978). Code of the Quipu: Databook.

prior_work.claim: Pre-formatted template khipus with high empty-cord fractions are attested at small-to-medium scale (UR273A 74 percent, UR192 54 percent, UR273B 73 percent, HP019 55 percent). Large-scale pre-formatted templates are rare.

v3_contribution.claim: CORPUS-RECORD ASCHER CASCADE DEPTH 6: exceeds AS092's depth 5 (previous corpus maximum). AS050 has a 6-level hierarchical Merkle-tree cross-verification structure, the deepest audit arithmetic in the analysed corpus.

evidence.test: max cascade depth ranking across analysed corpus

v3_contribution.claim: LARGE + SPARSE + DEEP + AUDIT-VERIFIED unique combination: 370 cords (3rd-largest) + 57 percent empty + cascade depth 6 + 88 percent Ascher verification. No other analysed document combines these 4 features. Structurally unique in the corpus.

SINGLE-TAQA BOUNDARY ANCHOR: 1 STRING across 370 cords (0.27 percent STRING density) + cadastral boundary marker = register anchored by a single cadastral concept with massive numerical scaffolding. Extreme lexical minimalism at scale.

LARGE-SCALE PRE-FORMATTED TEMPLATE: 210 empty / 370 cords = 57 percent at 370-cord scale. Largest-scale pre-formatted-template register in the corpus. Smaller-scale precedents (UR273A, UR192, UR273B at 70-124 cord scale) do not approach this scale.

55 DECLARED PENDANT-PENDANT SUMS is among the highest in corpus: matches or exceeds large-scale audit documents. 42 verified at 88 percent provides audit-grade cross-verification at the 6-level cascade, consistent with rigorous hierarchical administrative accounting.

evidence.test: pendant-pendant-sum count ranking + 88 percent verification rate

companions.note: Gaffron 10x6 audit matrix (56 cords, 18/18 = 100 percent Ascher, cascade depth 5). AS050 (this, depth 6 corpus record) + AS092 (depth 5 previous record) = top-2 deepest Merkle trees in the analysed corpus. Different scale (370 vs 56) but same audit-grade principle.
Incahuasi sparse event register (74 percent empty, 87 cords, no Ascher). AS050 scales up the sparse-template-register convention by 4x on cord count + adds deep audit verification.
Incahuasi audit totals (124 cords, 73 percent empty, peak 3991). AS050's sparse + audit-verified combination parallels UR273B but at 3x scale + depth 6 vs UR273B's no Ascher layer.
Huaca Perez tambo admin (70 cords, 54 percent empty, log-linear ascending). Pre-formatted register peer at smaller scale.
Pachacamac site-verification card (22 cords, 55 percent empty, chiqa x3). Compact pre-formatted-template analogue; AS050 is the 17x larger version.
Incahuasi UE 19 peanut audit (5/5 Ascher = 100 percent, 47-constant). Audit-grade peer; UR275 audits commodity at small scale, AS050 audits events at large sparse scale.

word_reading.note: 1/1 STRING at 100 percent dict hit (taqa). Single lexical anchor unambiguous.

document_type.note: event_register audit-verified sub-type robustly supported by (a) 88 percent Ascher verification, (b) corpus-record cascade depth 6, (c) 57 percent empty pre-formatted-template structure, (d) single taqa boundary anchor, (e) large-scale admin register framework. Sub-type resolution between event / audit / compound-component remains open without further provenance context.

narrative.note: The 'large-scale sparse audit-verified event register anchored by a boundary' reading is internally coherent and arithmetically anchored. Specific subject / provenance / time-span undetermined.

data_integrity.note: 88 percent Ascher verified = audit-grade. Cascade depth 6 (corpus record). 1 Pass 3C propagation. 57 percent empty structural not damage. KFG KH0061 catalogued. One of the best-documented audit profiles in the corpus despite unknown provenance.

sections.title: Large-Scale Pre-Formatted Event Register
sections.reading: 370-cord register organised into 17 clusters (avg 22 cords each). 159 INT + 210 EMPTY + 1 STRING (taqa boundary anchor). 5-color palette with B dominant (49 percent). 55 pendant-pendant sums with 42/48 headers verified (88 percent = audit-grade) at cascade depth 6 = deepest Merkle tree in the analysed corpus. 1 Pass 3C propagation.

key_cords.note: closes the 370-cord sparse audit-verified event register

references: Sivan, J. (2026). The Khipu as a Layered Information System. ALBA Project preprint.
Sivan, J. (2026). Ascher Sums as Erasure Codes. ALBA Project preprint.
Ascher, M. and Ascher, R. (1978). Code of the Quipu: Databook.
